lines.target: This land is your land, this land is my land, from California to the New York islands...
lines.base: Esta tierra es la vuestra, esta tierra es la mía, desde California hasta las islas de New York...

lines.target: So sang Woody Guthrie, and millions of people both American and non-American echo his sentiments about this most fascinating of countries.
lines.base: Así cantó Woody Guthrie, y muchos millones de gentes, americanos o no, se hacen eco de estos sentimientos con relación al más apasionante de los países.

lines.target: Its influence is felt throughout the world, but America is a land of such diversity and of so many contrasts, that it is impossible to think of the United States as a single entity.
lines.base: Su influencia se siente por todas partes del mundo, pero Norteamérica es un país de tal diversidad, de tantos contrastes, que es imposible considerar los Estados Unidos como una entidad única.

lines.target: From the golden beaches of California, through the sun-baked deserts of Arizona,
lines.base: Desde las playas doradas de California, hasta los desiertos resecos por el sol de Arizona,

lines.target: across the vast, flat plains of the Middle West, to the covered bridges of Vermont
lines.base: a través de las vastas llanuras planas del centro oeste,

lines.target: and the eclectic atmosphere and towering skyscrapers of New York:
lines.base: hasta los puentes cubiertos de Vermont

lines.target: of scenery and cultures that leave the traveler with a host of impressions and opinions.

lines.base: de paisajes y de culturas, que dejan al viajero con una multitud de impresiones y de opiniones contradictorias.

lines.target: glimpse of the land that opened its gates: Ellis Island in New York Sound.
lines.base: en un lugar donde tanta gente ha visto por primera y a veces por última vez

lines.target: Popularly known as the Isle of Tears, Ellis Island was the processing center
lines.base: la tierra que abrió sus puertas a los «fatigados y pobres»: Ellis Island, en el estrecho de New York.

lines.base: los inmigrantes que recién desembarcaban procedentes del Viejo Mundo.

lines.target: enter the country and start a new life?
lines.base: donde se iba a tomar la decisión fatídica: ¿podrían o no entrar al país y comenzar una nueva vida?

lines.target: Age, infirmity, a criminal record — any one of these was enough to send
lines.base: La edad, la enfermedad, o los antecedentes penales — solo hacía falta uno de estos elementos

lines.base: para que el inmigrante lleno de esperanza fuera enviado a su punto de partida.

lines.base: Otras personas, más afortunadas, pudieron entrar, pero a veces con un nuevo nombre

lines.base: debido a que los funcionarios de inmigración eran incapaces de leer o de pronunciar los nombres extranjeros.

lines.target: All this went on under the gaze of the symbol of the United States: the Statue of Liberty Guiding the World.
lines.base: Todo esto ocurría bajo la mirada del símbolo de los Estados Unidos: la estatua de la Libertad que guía al mundo.

lines.base: El viajero de hoy día, que llega por avión al aeropuerto de Kennedy, en New York ni siquiera se da cuenta de la isla;

lines.base: Este es un buen sistema para comenzar a comprender cómo se fueron formando los Estados Unidos de América.

lines.target: It is a good way to begin to understand the making of the United States of America.

gap_candidates.target: This land is your land, this land is my land, from California to the New York islands...
So sang Woody Guthrie, and millions of people both American and non-American echo his sentiments about this most fascinating of countries.
Its influence is felt throughout the world, but America is a land of such diversity and of so many contrasts, that it is impossible to think of the United States as a single entity.
From the golden beaches of California, through the sun-baked deserts of Arizona,
across the vast, flat plains of the Middle West, to the covered bridges of Vermont
and the eclectic atmosphere and towering skyscrapers of New York:
a journey across America is a journey through a bewildering array
of scenery and cultures that leave the traveler with a host of impressions and opinions.
This lesson is not intended to be a travelogue, so let's stop for a moment
at a place where so many people have seen their first — and sometimes last —
glimpse of the land that opened its gates: Ellis Island in New York Sound.
Popularly known as the Isle of Tears, Ellis Island was the processing center
for newly arrived immigrants from the Old World.
From 1892 to 1943, would-be new Americans had to pass through this place
where the fateful decision was made: could they or could they not
enter the country and start a new life?
Age, infirmity, a criminal record — any one of these was enough to send
immigrants back to where they had come from.
Others, more fortunate, were allowed to enter, but often with a new name
because the immigration officials were unable to read or to pronounce the foreign names.
All this went on under the gaze of the symbol of the United States: the Statue of Liberty Guiding the World.
Today's traveler jets into New York's JFK airport without even noticing the island;
but it is worth taking the time to visit the Isle of Tears, and to imagine yourself
arriving there one hundred years ago
in the hope of starting afresh in the New World.
It is a good way to begin to understand the making of the United States of America.
